description: Baseline: total gjq median time across all 13 benchmarks (quick mode, 10 iters)

asi.breakdown: randomusers queries dominate (13-15ms each), small files ~2.3ms each
asi.bottleneck: Large file (randomusers.json ~1MB) queries are the biggest contributors

description: Eliminate convertNumbers() pass: remove UseNumber(), use standard json.Decoder (float64 for all numbers). Saves a full tree walk + re-allocation of all maps/slices.

asi.hypothesis: Removing convertNumbers() pass would save ~15ms on 1MB file by avoiding tree re-walk and re-allocation
asi.result: 22% improvement: 65.70ms → 51.25ms total. randomusers queries dropped from 12-15ms to 9.5-10ms
asi.bottleneck: Remaining time is split between JSON parsing (~8ms for 1MB) and process overhead (~2ms)

description: Tried -ldflags="-s -w" build flags for stripped binary

asi.hypothesis: Stripping debug symbols with -ldflags would reduce binary size and speed up process startup
asi.result: No improvement, slightly worse: 51.25→55.97ms. macOS binary loading may not benefit from stripped symbols.
asi.next_action_hint: Focus on algorithmic optimizations instead of build flags

description: Inlined DFA hot path: direct field lookup, inline transition table access, fast-path for single range index lookup

asi.hypothesis: Inlining DFA field/transition lookups would reduce function call overhead in hot traverse loop
asi.next_action_hint: The issue may be the strings.ToLower call or code size. Try a different approach: optimize just FieldSymbolID by avoiding strings.ToLower allocation
asi.result: Worse: 51.25→60.98ms. Inlining made code larger and likely hurt instruction cache.

description: Pre-allocate results slice and fast-path single range in IndexSymbolID

asi.hypothesis: Pre-allocating results slice and fast-pathing single range in IndexSymbolID would reduce overhead
asi.next_action_hint: Focus on the JSON parsing layer instead - that's where the bulk of time is spent. Consider reducing Go runtime overhead.
asi.result: No improvement: 51.25→60.68ms. Changes didn't help and may have affected binary layout.

description: Tried debug.SetGCPercent(-1) to disable GC for short-lived CLI process

asi.hypothesis: Disabling GC for short-lived CLI process would reduce overhead
asi.next_action_hint: Try streaming JSON parsing to avoid building full tree in memory
asi.result: No improvement: GOGC=off via env var or debug.SetGCPercent(-1) had no measurable effect. The GC overhead is negligible for 1MB files.

description: Use ParseReader(file) instead of os.ReadFile + ParseBytes to avoid intermediate buffer

asi.hypothesis: Streaming JSON parsing via io.Reader would avoid file read into []byte buffer
asi.next_action_hint: Focus on more impactful optimizations like streaming DFA traversal
asi.result: No improvement: ~61ms same as before. json.Decoder already buffers internally, so removing the []byte intermediate doesn't help.
